commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix all SWEPs that not respect `SWEP.CanShootWhileRunning` (#70)

* Fix all SWEPs that not respect `SWEP.CanShootWhileRunning`
Now all M9K SWEPs definitely support this, and in the future, anyone who wants to disable this mechanic can simply do:
```lua
hook.Add("PreRegisterSWEP", "Example", function(swep, class)
	if class == "bobs_gun_base" then
		swep.CanShootWhileRunning = true
	end
end)
```
Perhaps these checks should be made a separate function after such a deep implementation, but overall, i think this is ok

commit.message:
major refactor to eliminate circular imports and modernize package

* Create dhtypes.py: consolidated domain classes with no circular imports

Combines all domain classes from dhcharts.py and dhtrades.py into a
single module that has no circular import dependencies.

Changes:
- New file dhtypes.py with Symbol, Candle, Chart, Event, Day,
  IndicatorDataPoint, Indicator, IndicatorSMA, IndicatorEMA (from
  dhcharts.py) and Trade, TradeSeries, Backtest (from dhtrades.py)
- Storage operations delegated via lazy sys.modules proxy functions
  so dhstore is not required at import time
- Removed .store() methods from all domain classes (storage ops
  are handled by dhstore module when imported separately)
- Symbol.market_is_open() no longer calls get_events() at import
  time; callers pass events list or get empty list by default
- Fixed Backtest.get_autoclose_time_by_date(): dt.combine() and
  dt.today() corrected for 'import datetime as dt' style
- Fixed Backtest.close_if_past_autoclose(): trade.is_closed ->
  trade.is_open with correct logic
- Updated class error messages to reference dhtypes instead of dhcharts
- All lines <= 79 chars (PEP 8), no trailing whitespace, LF endings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix review comments in dhtypes.py

- Move IndicatorDataPoint class docstring to correct position
- Move IndicatorEMA class docstring to correct position
- Fix typo: Sublcass -> Subclass in IndicatorEMA docstring
- Fix missing space in TypeError messages for d_symbol and c checks
- Fix 'obj' abbreviation to 'object' in Trade.update() TypeError

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix review comments in dhtypes.py restrict_dates and autoclose

- Replace self.store() calls in TradeSeries.restrict_dates() and
  Backtest.restrict_dates() with direct _dhstore() proxy calls since
  the .store() methods were removed from these classes
- Fix get_autoclose_time_by_date() to use candle_date instead of
  dt.datetime.today() when combining date with close_time

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: eliminate circular imports by creating dhtypes.py

- Create dhtypes.py consolidating all domain classes from dhcharts.py
  and dhtrades.py (Symbol, Candle, Chart, Event, Day, IndicatorDataPoint,

- Update dhstore.py: import from dhtypes instead of dhcharts/dhtrades

- Add CANDLE_TIMEFRAMES, BEGINNING_OF_TIME, MARKET_ERAS, bot() to
  dhcommon.py so callers can import them from there
- Update __init__.py with full public API (dhstore imports wrapped in
  try/except for environments without pymongo)
- Update all test files and testdata files to import from dhtypes
  instead of dhcharts/dhtrades

mappings so CI workflow templates derive values from project.yaml instead

commit.message:
v1.4.5: add delayed-render retry for Win+Shift+S screenshots

Win+Shift+S increments the clipboard sequence number immediately but
the Snipping Tool image isn't ready yet (~300-500ms later). When all
three priorities in _process_clipboard (image / files / text) fail to
match, schedule a single QTimer.singleShot(500ms) retry.

_retry_empty_clipboard():
  - Checks saved seq == current seq (no other app wrote clipboard)
  - Re-reads mime.hasImage() / clipboard.image()
  - Captures if valid; deduplicates via _image_hash
  - Fires at most once per original clipboard event (_retry_seq guard)

No changes to image capture logic, threading, or other features.
All 8 integration tests pass.

commit.message:
[Feature] azd update command, auto-update, and channel management (#6942)

* Add hidden azd update command with auto-update and channel management

Implements azd update (epic #6721) as a hidden command behind the
alpha.update feature toggle (default: off).

Features:
- Manual update via 'azd update' with install-method-aware strategy
  (brew/winget/choco delegation, direct binary download for scripts)
- Channel switching (stable/daily) with downgrade confirmation
- Auto-update: background staging + apply on next startup with re-exec
- SHA256 checksum and code signing verification (macOS/Windows)
- Elevation-aware auto-update (graceful fallback to warning for
  system installs, silent apply for user-writable locations)
- Daily version tracking via single-line version.txt with build number
  parsed from the daily.N suffix
- CI detection to skip auto-update in pipelines
- 13 telemetry error codes integrated into MapError pipeline
- azd version shows channel suffix when feature is enabled
- Zero behavior change when alpha.update is off

Pipeline changes:
- Daily publish writes version.txt with full version string
- SHA256 checksum generation for binary downloads

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle Linux ETXTBSY in copyFile for auto-update apply

On Linux, os.Create fails with ETXTBSY when overwriting a running
executable. Fix copyFile to unlink the old file first (the running
process retains its fd via the inode), then create a new file at the
same path. If the unlink fails with permission denied (e.g. root-owned
directory), return the permission error so ApplyStagedUpdate can
trigger the elevation/keep-staged path.

Also preserve source file permissions via os.Chmod after remove-then-
create, since the new file would otherwise get default 0666 permissions
instead of the original executable 0755.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Block azd update for dev builds (0.0.0-dev.0)

Dev builds should not self-update — developers build from source.
Returns a clear error message instead of silently replacing the
dev binary with a release build.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix MSI self-update by spawning msiexec detached

msiexec cannot replace a locked executable. Previously azd ran msiexec as a child process and waited. msiexec would kill the parent azd before completing, leaving the binary unreplaced.

Now msiexec is spawned detached with CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP and DETACHED_PROCESS on Windows so azd can exit normally and release the binary lock. msiexec then completes the replacement independently.

Always writes verbose MSI log to ~/.azd/logs/msi-update.log for diagnosability. Debug mode shows the log path and msiexec command line.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove misleading alpha toggle hint from azd update

The auto-enable logic re-enables the alpha feature on every run, so telling users to unset it is confusing since it has no effect.

feat(build): enable npm pinning enforcement in dependency scan (#838)

## Description

Enabled **npm pinning enforcement** in the CI dependency-pinning scan
workflow. Updated the workflow default to scan
`github-actions,npm,workflow-npm-commands`, activating automatic
detection of unpinned npm commands in GitHub Actions workflow `run:`
blocks.

Three PowerShell functions were added to *Test-DependencyPinning.ps1*:

- **`Test-NpmCommandLine`** — regex-based detection of unpinned npm
commands (`npm install`, `npm i`, `npm update`, `npm install-test`)
while excluding safe variants (`npm ci`, `npm run`, `npm test`, `npm
audit`)
- **`Get-WorkflowNpmCommandViolations`** — indentation-aware YAML parser
that tracks nesting depth to confine detection to actual shell content
and avoid flagging comments
- **`New-NpmCommandViolation`** — constructs violation objects with
Medium severity and remediation guidance

Pester test coverage was added with fixture files for both safe and
unsafe npm command patterns.

### Scope Change: npm Exact-Version Enforcement

This PR introduces a **strategic departure** from SHA-pinning for npm
dependencies. Unlike GitHub Actions (which use 40-character commit SHAs
for immutable references), npm dependencies use **exact-version
enforcement** (`X.Y.Z` with no `^`, `~`, `*`, or range operators).

**Rationale for exact-version enforcement over SHA-pinning:**

| Factor | GitHub Actions (SHA) | npm (Exact Version) |
|--------|---------------------|---------------------|
| Registry model | Git repositories with mutable tags | Immutable
registry artifacts |
| Integrity verification | SHA pins to specific commit | Lockfile
integrity hashes (SHA-512) |
| Audit compatibility | N/A | `npm audit` requires semver versions |
| Reproducibility | SHA guarantees exact code | Lockfile + exact version
guarantees exact code |
| Human readability | 40-char hex strings | Semantic version numbers |

The validation regex enforces exact versions: `^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$` — no
ranges, no prefixes, no wildcards.

### Follow-Up Hardening (commit `b8bd099`)

Additional improvements shipped on this branch:

- **pip ExcludePatterns** — Virtual environment directories (`.venv`,
`venv`, `.tox`, `.nox`, `__pypackages__`) are now excluded from pip
dependency scanning to avoid false positives
- **SARIF integration tests** — Expanded from 1 weak assertion to 8
focused tests validating SARIF 2.1.0 schema compliance, severity mapping
(High→error, Medium→warning), location structure, and metadata
properties
- **Dependency pinning documentation** — New
`docs/security/dependency-pinning.md` page documenting all pinning
strategies with rationale, validation rules, CI integration flowchart,
and a dedicated comparison of SHA-pinning vs exact-version enforcement
for npm

refactor(parser): omit title key when undefined; add codeNode() helper

push sites used shorthand `{ ..., title }`, which always writes the key
even when title === undefined, making the object `{ title: undefined }`
rather than simply absent. deepStrictEqual() distinguishes these.

Add a codeNode(lang, text, title?) factory that only includes the title
key when the value is a string. Replace all six push sites with it.

Remove the now-redundant `title: undefined` from 44 expected objects in
parser.test.ts and renderer.test.ts (test refactor — the assertions still
verify the same behaviour, just without coupling to the undefined-key
